evals.prompt: How do I create a server API route in Nuxt 3 that handles POST requests with body validation?
evals.expected_output: Guide through creating server/api/ file with defineEventHandler, readBody, and validation
expectations: Creates file in server/api/ directory with .post.ts suffix for POST-only handling
Uses readBody(event) to parse request body
Includes input validation (with zod, H3 validators, or manual checks)
Shows proper error handling with createError()

evals.prompt: I need to add authentication middleware that redirects unauthenticated users to the login page in my Nuxt 3 app.
evals.expected_output: Guide through creating route middleware with defineNuxtRouteMiddleware and navigateTo
expectations: Creates middleware file in middleware/ directory
Uses defineNuxtRouteMiddleware (or direct export default function)
Checks authentication state (via useState, useCookie, or composable)
Shows how to apply middleware via definePageMeta or as global middleware

evals.prompt: How do I fetch data from an API in Nuxt 3 with proper SSR support and loading states?
evals.expected_output: Guide through useFetch or useAsyncData with SSR considerations, loading/error handling
expectations: Uses useFetch() or useAsyncData() for SSR-compatible data fetching
Shows destructured return values: data, pending, error, refresh
Explains the difference between useFetch and useAsyncData
Addresses key-based caching and when to use a unique key
